summary: Excellent audience‑mic mix captures a strong second set highlighted by Way to Go Home, Terrapin Station → The Wheel jam and a surprise Dark Star.
review: The four‑mic Sennheiser audience recording is crisp and well balanced, avoiding the typical FOB handclap clutter. Musically the show shines in its second half – Jerry’s lead on Way to Go Home, an inspired Terrapin Station jam that flows into The Wheel, plus a rare Dark Star and energetic Good Lovin'. Overall the performance is praised as one of the better Deer Creek dates of ’93.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers give 5‑star ratings and highlight superb playing and a high‑quality audience recording; the limited number of reviews keeps confidence at medium.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Four‑mic Sennheiser mix (ME‑80 shotguns + 421 cardioids) provides a tight sound field with clear ambience and no intrusive handclaps or crowd cheer.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as a bit lackluster, but the second set erupts with strong energy – Samson opens, Way to Go Home dazzles, a unique Terrapin Station jam segues into an unexpected Dark Star and then The Wheel, closing with Good Lovin' and Lucy in the Sky.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Lead guitar on Way to Go Home praised for relaxed yet confident solos; also noted throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums receive high praise, especially during the Terrapin Station → The Wheel jam and overall space sections.
